tagline: Estimate bulk AI image generation costs across platforms
keywords: AI image bulk cost, DALL-E pricing, Midjourney cost, Stable Diffusion, AI art batch
calcFn: var prices = {'DALL-E 3': {'Standard (1024x1024)': 0.04, 'HD (1792x1024)': 0.08, '4K Upscale': 0.12}, 'Midjourney': {'Standard (1024x1024)': 0.02, 'HD (1792x1024)': 0.04, '4K Upscale': 0.06}, 'Stable Diffusion (API)': {'Standard (1024x1024)': 0.002, 'HD (1792x1024)': 0.006, '4K Upscale': 0.01}, 'Adobe Firefly': {'Standard (1024x1024)': 0.03, 'HD (1792x1024)': 0.06, '4K Upscale': 0.10}}; var cpp = prices[platform][resolution] || 0.04; var monthlyCost = cpp * images; var annualCost = monthlyCost * 12; return {costPerImage: dollar(cpp), monthlyCost: dollar(monthlyCost), annualCost: dollar(annualCost)};

faq.q: Which AI image platform is cheapest?
faq.a: Stable Diffusion API is typically cheapest per image, while DALL-E 3 offers the best prompt adherence at a higher price.

faq.q: Can I run AI image generation locally for free?
faq.a: Yes, Stable Diffusion can run locally on a GPU with 8GB+ VRAM, eliminating per-image costs after hardware investment.

formulas.name: Monthly Cost
formulas.formula: Monthly Cost = Images × Cost per Image
formulas.explanation: Total images generated multiplied by the per-image rate for your platform and resolution.

tagline: Estimate costs to label training data for ML models
keywords: data labeling cost, annotation cost, machine learning data, training data, AI dataset
calcFn: var rates = {'Text Classification': {'In-house Team': 0.10, 'Crowdsource (MTurk)': 0.03, 'Managed Service': 0.08, 'AI-Assisted + Review': 0.02}, 'Image Bounding Box': {'In-house Team': 0.25, 'Crowdsource (MTurk)': 0.08, 'Managed Service': 0.15, 'AI-Assisted + Review': 0.05}, 'Image Segmentation': {'In-house Team': 0.80, 'Crowdsource (MTurk)': 0.25, 'Managed Service': 0.50, 'AI-Assisted + Review': 0.12}, 'Named Entity Recognition': {'In-house Team': 0.15, 'Crowdsource (MTurk)': 0.05, 'Managed Service': 0.10, 'AI-Assisted + Review': 0.03}, 'Audio Transcription': {'In-house Team': 0.50, 'Crowdsource (MTurk)': 0.15, 'Managed Service': 0.30, 'AI-Assisted + Review': 0.08}}; var cps = rates[taskType][method] || 0.10; var totalCost = cps * samples; var samplesPerWeek = method === 'In-house Team' ? 2000 : method === 'Crowdsource (MTurk)' ? 10000 : method === 'Managed Service' ? 5000 : 15000; var timeEstimate = Math.ceil(samples / samplesPerWeek); return {costPerSample: dollar(cps), totalCost: dollar(totalCost), timeEstimate: fmt(timeEstimate, 0)};

faq.q: What is the cheapest way to label training data?
faq.a: AI-assisted labeling with human review is cheapest and fastest, using a pre-trained model to generate initial labels that humans then correct.

faq.q: How much labeled data do I need for a good ML model?
faq.a: It varies widely: text classifiers can work with 1,000-5,000 samples, while image segmentation models often need 10,000+ labeled examples.

formulas.name: Total Labeling Cost
formulas.formula: Total Cost = Number of Samples x Cost per Sample
formulas.explanation: Multiply your dataset size by the per-sample rate based on task complexity and labeling method.

tagline: Estimate deployment and interaction gas costs for smart contracts
keywords: smart contract gas, Ethereum deployment cost, Solidity gas, contract deploy cost, gas optimization
calcFn: var deployGas = contractSize * 200 + 21000; var deployCostEth = (deployGas * gasPrice) / 1e9; var deployCost = deployCostEth * ethPrice; var avgCallGas = 65000; var dailyGas = avgCallGas * functions; var dailyCostEth = (dailyGas * gasPrice) / 1e9; var dailyInteractionCost = dailyCostEth * ethPrice; var monthlyCost = dailyInteractionCost * 30; return {deployGas: fmt(deployGas, 0), deployCost: dollar(deployCost), dailyInteractionCost: dollar(dailyInteractionCost), monthlyCost: dollar(monthlyCost)};

faq.q: How much does it cost to deploy a smart contract?
faq.a: Deployment costs depend on contract size and gas prices. A typical ERC-20 token contract costs $50-$500 at moderate gas prices.

faq.q: How can I reduce smart contract gas costs?
faq.a: Optimize storage usage, use events instead of storage where possible, batch operations, and consider Layer 2 solutions like Arbitrum or Optimism.

formulas.name: Gas Cost
formulas.formula: Cost (USD) = Gas Units x Gas Price (Gwei) / 1e9 x ETH Price
formulas.explanation: Convert gas units to ETH using the gas price, then multiply by the current ETH/USD exchange rate.

tagline: Calculate potential returns and impermanent loss from yield farming
keywords: yield farming, DeFi returns, liquidity pool, APY calculator, farming rewards
calcFn: var months = duration; var poolRate = poolApy / 100; var rewardRate = rewardToken / 100; var poolEarnings = deposit * (Math.pow(1 + poolRate / 12, months) - 1); var rewardEarnings = deposit * (rewardRate / 12) * months; var totalGas = gasFees * months; var netProfit = poolEarnings + rewardEarnings - totalGas; var effectiveApy = (netProfit / deposit) * (12 / months) * 100; return {poolEarnings: dollar(poolEarnings), rewardEarnings: dollar(rewardEarnings), totalGas: dollar(totalGas), netProfit: dollar(netProfit), effectiveApy: fmt(effectiveApy, 1)};

faq.q: Is yield farming profitable after gas fees?
faq.a: It depends on deposit size and gas costs. Small deposits under $1,000 often lose money to fees, especially on Ethereum mainnet.

faq.q: What risks does yield farming have?
faq.a: Key risks include impermanent loss, smart contract bugs, rug pulls, and token price crashes. Never invest more than you can afford to lose.

formulas.name: Effective APY
formulas.formula: Effective APY = (Net Profit / Deposit) x (12 / Months) x 100
formulas.explanation: Annualized return after subtracting gas fees from total earnings.

tagline: Estimate earnings from running a blockchain validator node
keywords: validator earnings, staking rewards, node operator, Ethereum validator, proof of stake
calcFn: var grossEth = stakeAmount * (networkApr / 100) * (uptime / 100); var annualUsd = grossEth * ethPrice; var annualCosts = electricityCost * 12; var monthlyNet = (annualUsd - annualCosts) / 12; var effectiveApr = ((annualUsd - annualCosts) / (stakeAmount * ethPrice)) * 100; return {annualEth: fmt(grossEth, 4), annualUsd: dollar(annualUsd), monthlyNet: dollar(monthlyNet), effectiveApr: fmt(effectiveApr, 2)};

faq.q: How much do Ethereum validators earn?
faq.a: At current rates, a 32 ETH validator earns roughly 4-5% APR, translating to about 1.3-1.6 ETH per year before costs.

faq.q: What hardware do I need to run a validator?
faq.a: A modest setup works: 4+ core CPU, 16GB RAM, 2TB SSD, and a reliable internet connection. Total hardware cost is typically $500-$1,500.

formulas.name: Net Validator Income
formulas.formula: Net = (Stake x APR x Uptime) x ETH Price - Annual Costs
formulas.explanation: Gross staking rewards adjusted for uptime and reduced by operating costs.

tagline: Size your solar battery bank for off-grid or backup power
keywords: solar battery size, battery bank calculator, off-grid battery, energy storage, solar storage
calcFn: var usableKwh = dailyUsage * backupDays; var totalKwh = usableKwh / (depthOfDischarge / 100); var voltMap = {'12V': 12, '24V': 24, '48V': 48}; var volts = voltMap[batteryVoltage] || 48; var ampHours = (totalKwh * 1000) / volts; var costPerKwh = {'Lithium (LFP)': 400, 'Lead-Acid (AGM)': 250, 'Lead-Acid (Flooded)': 150}; var estimatedCost = totalKwh * (costPerKwh[batteryType] || 400); return {totalKwh: fmt(totalKwh, 1), usableKwh: fmt(usableKwh, 1), ampHours: fmt(ampHours, 0), estimatedCost: dollar(estimatedCost)};

faq.q: How many batteries do I need for off-grid solar?
faq.a: It depends on your daily usage and backup days. A typical home using 30 kWh/day needs 60-75 kWh of battery capacity for 2 days of autonomy.

faq.q: Lithium vs lead-acid for solar storage?
faq.a: Lithium (LFP) costs more upfront but lasts 10+ years with deeper discharge. Lead-acid is cheaper initially but needs replacement every 3-5 years.

formulas.name: Battery Capacity
formulas.formula: Total kWh = Daily Usage x Backup Days / Depth of Discharge
formulas.explanation: The total battery capacity accounts for the fact that you should not fully discharge batteries.

tagline: Calculate return on investment for installing EV chargers
keywords: EV charger ROI, charging station investment, EV charger cost, Level 2 charger, DCFC investment

inputs.label: Avg Sessions per Day per Charger

calcFn: var installPrices = {'Level 2 (7-19 kW)': 6000, 'DC Fast (50 kW)': 50000, 'DC Fast (150 kW)': 120000}; var installCost = (installPrices[chargerType] || 6000) * numChargers; var dailyKwh = sessionsPerDay * avgKwhPerSession * numChargers; var monthlyRevenue = dailyKwh * rateCharged * 30; var monthlyElectricity = dailyKwh * electricityCost * 30; var monthlyMaintenance = numChargers * 50; var monthlyProfit = monthlyRevenue - monthlyElectricity - monthlyMaintenance; var paybackMonths = monthlyProfit > 0 ? Math.ceil(installCost / monthlyProfit) : 0; return {installCost: dollar(installCost), monthlyRevenue: dollar(monthlyRevenue), monthlyProfit: dollar(monthlyProfit), paybackMonths: paybackMonths > 0 ? fmt(paybackMonths, 0) : 'N/A'};

faq.q: How much does it cost to install an EV charger?
faq.a: Level 2 chargers cost $3,000-$10,000 installed. DC fast chargers range from $30,000-$150,000+ depending on power output.

faq.q: Are EV charging stations profitable?
faq.a: In high-traffic locations with 4+ sessions/day, Level 2 chargers can pay back in 2-3 years. DC fast chargers need higher utilization but earn more per session.

formulas.name: Payback Period
formulas.formula: Payback = Install Cost / Monthly Net Profit
formulas.explanation: Total investment divided by monthly revenue minus electricity and maintenance costs.

tagline: Estimate the cost of GDPR, CCPA, or other data privacy compliance
keywords: GDPR compliance cost, CCPA cost, data privacy, privacy regulation, compliance budget
calcFn: var baseCosts = {'GDPR (EU)': 50000, 'CCPA/CPRA (California)': 30000, 'HIPAA (Healthcare)': 80000, 'SOC 2': 60000, 'Multiple Frameworks': 120000}; var sizeMultiplier = employees < 50 ? 0.5 : employees < 200 ? 1 : employees < 1000 ? 2 : 4; var volumeMultiplier = {'Under 10,000': 0.7, '10,000 - 100,000': 1, '100,000 - 1 million': 1.5, 'Over 1 million': 2.5}; var stateMultiplier = {'Starting from Scratch': 1.5, 'Partially Compliant': 1, 'Mostly Compliant': 0.4}; var initialCost = baseCosts[regulation] * sizeMultiplier * (volumeMultiplier[dataVolume] || 1) * (stateMultiplier[currentState] || 1); var annualMaintenance = initialCost * 0.3; var dpoSalary = employees > 100 ? 120000 : 0; var penalties = {'GDPR (EU)': 20000000, 'CCPA/CPRA (California)': 7500, 'HIPAA (Healthcare)': 1500000, 'SOC 2': 0, 'Multiple Frameworks': 20000000}; var penaltyRisk = penalties[regulation] || 0; return {initialCost: dollar(initialCost), annualMaintenance: dollar(annualMaintenance), dpoSalary: dpoSalary > 0 ? dollar(dpoSalary) : 'N/A (under 100 employees)', penaltyRisk: penaltyRisk > 0 ? dollar(penaltyRisk) : 'Contractual only'};

faq.q: How much does GDPR compliance cost for a small business?
faq.a: Small businesses (under 50 employees) typically spend $15,000-$50,000 on initial compliance, with $5,000-$15,000 in annual maintenance.

faq.q: Do I need a Data Protection Officer?
faq.a: Under GDPR, a DPO is mandatory for organizations processing large-scale personal data or public authorities. Under CCPA, there is no DPO requirement but a privacy lead is recommended.

formulas.name: Initial Cost
formulas.formula: Cost = Base x Size Multiplier x Data Volume Multiplier x State Multiplier
formulas.explanation: The base compliance cost scaled by company size, data volume, and how much work remains.

tagline: Estimate costs for wills, trusts, and estate planning documents
keywords: estate planning cost, will cost, living trust cost, estate attorney, probate avoidance
calcFn: var basePrices = {'Simple Will': {'Online Service': 150, 'Attorney (Small Firm)': 500, 'Attorney (Large Firm)': 1200}, 'Living Trust': {'Online Service': 500, 'Attorney (Small Firm)': 2500, 'Attorney (Large Firm)': 5000}, 'Complex Trust (Irrevocable)': {'Online Service': 0, 'Attorney (Small Firm)': 5000, 'Attorney (Large Firm)': 12000}, 'Full Estate Plan': {'Online Service': 800, 'Attorney (Small Firm)': 4000, 'Attorney (Large Firm)': 10000}}; var complexityMult = {'Simple (single, few assets)': 1, 'Moderate (married, home + accounts)': 1.5, 'Complex (business, multiple properties)': 2.5}; var base = basePrices[planType][method] || 1000; if (planType === 'Complex Trust (Irrevocable)' && method === 'Online Service') base = 5000; var documentCost = base * (complexityMult[state] || 1); var annualMaintenance = planType === 'Simple Will' ? 0 : documentCost * 0.05; var probateRate = estateValue > 1000000 ? 0.04 : 0.05; var probateSavings = planType !== 'Simple Will' ? estateValue * probateRate : 0; var totalFirstYear = documentCost + annualMaintenance; return {documentCost: dollar(documentCost), annualMaintenance: annualMaintenance > 0 ? dollar(annualMaintenance) : 'None', probateSavings: probateSavings > 0 ? dollar(probateSavings) : 'N/A (will goes through probate)', totalFirstYear: dollar(totalFirstYear)};

faq.q: Is a living trust worth the cost?
faq.a: For estates over $100,000 or those with real estate, a living trust typically saves far more in probate costs and time than its upfront price.

faq.q: How often should I update my estate plan?
faq.a: Review every 3-5 years or after major life events: marriage, divorce, birth of a child, significant asset changes, or moving to a new state.

formulas.name: Probate Savings
formulas.formula: Savings = Estate Value x Probate Rate (4-5%)
formulas.explanation: Trusts bypass probate, saving the percentage that courts and attorneys typically charge.

tagline: Calculate palladium investment value and returns
keywords: palladium price, palladium investment, palladium value, PGM investment, palladium bullion
calcFn: var ozConvert = {'Troy Ounces': 1, 'Grams': 0.03215, 'Kilograms': 32.15}; var troyOz = weight * (ozConvert[weightUnit] || 1); var currentValue = troyOz * spotPrice; var totalWithPremium = troyOz * spotPrice * (1 + premium / 100); var costBasis = troyOz * purchasePrice; var gainLoss = currentValue - costBasis; var returnPct = costBasis > 0 ? (gainLoss / costBasis) * 100 : 0; return {currentValue: dollar(currentValue), totalWithPremium: dollar(totalWithPremium), gainLoss: dollar(gainLoss), returnPct: fmt(returnPct, 2)};

faq.q: Why invest in palladium?
faq.a: Palladium is rarer than gold and has strong industrial demand, especially for catalytic converters. It can diversify a precious metals portfolio beyond gold and silver.

faq.q: How does palladium compare to platinum?
faq.a: Palladium has outperformed platinum in recent years due to gasoline engine catalyst demand, while platinum is used more in diesel catalysts.

formulas.name: Current Value
formulas.formula: Value = Troy Ounces x Spot Price
formulas.explanation: Weight converted to troy ounces multiplied by the current spot price.

tagline: Compare costs for storing gold, silver, and other precious metals
keywords: gold storage cost, precious metal vault, bullion storage, allocated storage, depository fees
calcFn: var rates = {'Home Safe': 0, 'Bank Safe Deposit Box': 0.002, 'Private Vault (Segregated)': 0.005, 'Private Vault (Allocated)': 0.004}; var silverPremium = metalType === 'Silver' ? 1.5 : metalType === 'Mixed Portfolio' ? 1.2 : 1; var annualRate = rates[storageType] || 0; var annualCost = storageType === 'Home Safe' ? 0 : Math.max(metalValue * annualRate * silverPremium, storageType === 'Bank Safe Deposit Box' ? 150 : 200); var totalCost = annualCost * years; var costAsPct = (annualCost / metalValue) * 100; var insuranceRate = storageType === 'Home Safe' ? 0.015 : storageType === 'Bank Safe Deposit Box' ? 0.005 : 0.001; var insuranceCost = metalValue * insuranceRate; return {annualCost: storageType === 'Home Safe' ? 'Free (but see insurance)' : dollar(annualCost), totalCost: dollar(totalCost), costAsPct: fmt(costAsPct, 2), insuranceCost: dollar(insuranceCost)};

faq.q: What is the cheapest way to store precious metals?
faq.a: Home storage is cheapest but riskiest. For larger holdings ($50k+), a private vault with insurance is typically 0.4-0.5% per year and far more secure.

faq.q: Does silver cost more to store than gold?
faq.a: Yes, because silver has a lower value-to-weight ratio. Storing $50,000 in silver takes roughly 100x more space than $50,000 in gold.

formulas.name: Annual Storage Cost
formulas.formula: Cost = Metal Value x Annual Rate (with minimums)
formulas.explanation: Most vaults charge a percentage of value with minimum annual fees.

tagline: Calculate the true cost of each serving in your recipe
keywords: recipe cost, cost per serving, food cost, meal prep cost, ingredient cost
calcFn: var ingredientCost = ingredient1 + ingredient2 + ingredient3 + ingredient4 + ingredient5; var overhead = ingredientCost * (overheadPct / 100); var totalCost = ingredientCost + overhead; var costPerServing = totalCost / servings; var vsRestaurant = costPerServing * 3.5; var savings = vsRestaurant - costPerServing; return {totalCost: dollar(totalCost), costPerServing: dollar(costPerServing), vsRestaurant: dollar(vsRestaurant), savings: dollar(savings)};

faq.q: What is a reasonable cost per serving for home cooking?
faq.a: Most home-cooked meals cost $2-$5 per serving, compared to $12-$25 for the restaurant equivalent.

faq.q: How do I reduce my cost per serving?
faq.a: Buy in bulk, use seasonal produce, substitute expensive proteins, and cook larger batches for better per-serving economics.

formulas.name: Cost per Serving
formulas.formula: Cost per Serving = (Ingredient Costs + Overhead) / Servings
formulas.explanation: Total ingredient costs plus estimated overhead divided by the number of servings.

tagline: Estimate daily and monthly profit for a food truck business
keywords: food truck profit, food truck business, food truck revenue, mobile food, food truck costs
calcFn: var dailyRevenue = avgTicket * customersPerDay; var dailyFoodCost = dailyRevenue * (foodCostPct / 100); var dailyFuel = 25; var dailyOther = 30; var dailyProfit = dailyRevenue - dailyFoodCost - laborDaily - dailyFuel - dailyOther; var monthlyDays = daysPerWeek * 4.33; var monthlyRevenue = dailyRevenue * monthlyDays; var monthlyProfit = (dailyProfit * monthlyDays) - rent; var annualProfit = monthlyProfit * 12; return {dailyRevenue: dollar(dailyRevenue), dailyProfit: dollar(dailyProfit), monthlyProfit: dollar(monthlyProfit), annualProfit: dollar(annualProfit)};

faq.q: How much can a food truck make per year?
faq.a: Successful food trucks gross $250,000-$500,000 annually. After all expenses, net profit is typically $50,000-$150,000.

faq.q: What is a good food cost percentage for a food truck?
faq.a: Target 28-32% food cost. Below 25% may signal quality issues, above 35% squeezes margins too thin.

formulas.name: Daily Profit
formulas.formula: Profit = Revenue - Food Cost - Labor - Fuel - Other Expenses
formulas.explanation: Gross revenue minus all variable daily costs gives your daily operating profit.

tagline: Find compatible keys and BPM ranges for mixing songs
keywords: DJ mixing, harmonic mixing, Camelot wheel, BPM matching, key compatibility
calcFn: var camelot = {'C Major':'8B','C# Major':'3B','D Major':'10B','D# Major':'5B','E Major':'12B','F Major':'7B','F# Major':'2B','G Major':'9B','G# Major':'4B','A Major':'11B','A# Major':'6B','B Major':'1B','A Minor':'8A','A# Minor':'3A','B Minor':'10A','C Minor':'5A','C# Minor':'12A','D Minor':'7A','D# Minor':'2A','E Minor':'9A','F Minor':'4A','F# Minor':'11A','G Minor':'6A','G# Minor':'1A'}; var code = camelot[currentKey] || '8B'; var num = parseInt(code); var letter = code.slice(-1); var up = ((num % 12) + 1) || 12; var down = ((num - 2 + 12) % 12) + 1; var otherLetter = letter === 'A' ? 'B' : 'A'; var rev = {}; for (var k in camelot) { rev[camelot[k]] = k; } var compatible = [rev[num + letter], rev[up + letter], rev[down + letter], rev[num + otherLetter]].filter(function(v){return v;}); var bpmLow = Math.round(currentBpm * (1 - bpmRange / 100)); var bpmHigh = Math.round(currentBpm * (1 + bpmRange / 100)); var halfDouble = Math.round(currentBpm / 2) + ' / ' + Math.round(currentBpm * 2); return {compatibleKeys: compatible.join(', '), bpmLow: fmt(bpmLow, 0), bpmHigh: fmt(bpmHigh, 0), halfDouble: halfDouble};

faq.q: What is harmonic mixing?
faq.a: Harmonic mixing uses the Camelot wheel to blend songs in compatible musical keys, creating smoother transitions without clashing notes.

faq.q: How much can I adjust BPM when mixing?
faq.a: Staying within 3-5% BPM change sounds natural. Beyond 8% the pitch shift becomes noticeable without key lock.

formulas.name: BPM Range
formulas.formula: Range = BPM x (1 +/- Tolerance%)
formulas.explanation: The current tempo multiplied by your acceptable percentage range.

tagline: Calculate competitive rates for private music lessons
keywords: music lesson price, private lesson rate, music teacher pay, instrument lesson cost, music tutor rate
calcFn: var baseRates = {'Beginner (0-2 years)': 35, 'Intermediate (3-7 years)': 55, 'Advanced (8-15 years)': 75, 'Master/Degree (15+ years)': 100}; var lengthMult = {'30': 0.6, '45': 0.85, '60': 1, '90': 1.4}; var locationMult = {'In-Home (student)': 1.15, 'Your Studio': 1, 'Online': 0.9, 'Music School': 0.75}; var colMult = {'Low': 0.75, 'Medium': 1, 'High': 1.35, 'Very High (NYC, SF)': 1.7}; var base = baseRates[experience] || 55; var rate = base * (lengthMult[lessonLength] || 1) * (locationMult[location] || 1) * (colMult[costOfLiving] || 1); var hourlyEquivalent = rate / (parseInt(lessonLength) / 60); var monthlyIncome = rate * 20 * 4.33; var annualIncome = rate * 20 * 44; return {suggestedRate: dollar(rate), hourlyEquivalent: dollar(hourlyEquivalent), monthlyIncome: dollar(monthlyIncome), annualIncome: dollar(annualIncome)};

faq.q: How much should I charge for music lessons?
faq.a: Rates vary from $30-$120 per hour based on experience, location, and instrument. Research local competitors and price based on your qualifications.

faq.q: Should I charge less for online lessons?
faq.a: Online lessons typically go for 10-15% less since you have no travel time or studio overhead, but the convenience can justify full rates.

formulas.name: Lesson Rate
formulas.formula: Rate = Base x Length Multiplier x Location Multiplier x Cost-of-Living Multiplier
formulas.explanation: Your experience-based rate adjusted for lesson duration, teaching location, and local market.

tagline: Calculate your luggage weight and check airline limits
keywords: packing weight, luggage weight, carry-on limit, checked bag weight, airline baggage
calcFn: var avgClothingWeight = 0.6; var avgShoeWeight = 2; var clothingWeight = clothing * avgClothingWeight; var shoeWeight = shoes * avgShoeWeight; var totalWeight = clothingWeight + shoeWeight + toiletries + electronics + misc + bagWeight; var limits = {'Carry-on (22 lb limit)': 22, 'Checked Bag (50 lb limit)': 50, 'Checked Bag (70 lb limit)': 70}; var weightLimit = limits[bagType] || 50; var remaining = weightLimit - totalWeight; var status = remaining >= 10 ? 'Well under limit' : remaining >= 0 ? 'Close to limit' : 'OVER LIMIT by ' + fmt(Math.abs(remaining), 1) + ' lbs'; return {totalWeight: fmt(totalWeight, 1), weightLimit: fmt(weightLimit, 0), remaining: fmt(remaining, 1), status: status};

faq.q: How much does a typical outfit weigh?
faq.a: A casual outfit (shirt, pants, underwear, socks) averages about 1.5-2 lbs. Heavy items like jeans and sweaters add more.

faq.q: What is the heaviest item in most luggage?
faq.a: Shoes are usually the heaviest per-item. Wearing your bulkiest shoes on the plane saves significant bag weight.

formulas.name: Total Weight
formulas.formula: Total = Clothing + Shoes + Toiletries + Electronics + Misc + Bag Weight
formulas.explanation: Sum of all item weights plus the empty bag weight compared against airline limits.

tagline: Compare currency exchange options and hidden fees
keywords: currency exchange fee, travel money, forex fee, ATM abroad, money exchange cost
calcFn: var feeRates = {'Airport Kiosk': 0.10, 'Bank (pre-trip)': 0.03, 'ATM Abroad': 0.02, 'Credit Card (no foreign fee)': 0.005, 'Credit Card (3% fee)': 0.035, 'Hotel Exchange': 0.12}; var feeRate = feeRates[method] || 0.05; var atmFee = method === 'ATM Abroad' ? 5 : 0; var totalFees = (amount * feeRate) + atmFee; var netAmount = amount - totalFees; var effectiveRate = midMarketRate * (1 - feeRate); var youReceive = netAmount * midMarketRate; var costPer100 = (totalFees / amount) * 100; return {effectiveRate: fmt(effectiveRate, 4), youReceive: fmt(youReceive, 2), totalFees: dollar(totalFees), costPer100: dollar(costPer100)};

faq.q: What is the cheapest way to exchange currency for travel?
faq.a: A no-foreign-transaction-fee credit card gives the best rates (typically 0-0.5% markup). ATMs abroad are second best at 1-3% total cost.

faq.q: Should I exchange money before or during my trip?
faq.a: Use ATMs or credit cards at your destination for the best rates. Only exchange a small amount at home for immediate needs upon arrival.

formulas.name: Total Fees
formulas.formula: Fees = Amount x Fee Rate + Fixed Fees
formulas.explanation: The percentage markup charged by the exchange method plus any flat transaction fees.

tagline: Calculate round-trip fuel costs for weekend and day trips
keywords: day trip fuel cost, road trip gas, weekend trip cost, driving cost, trip fuel estimate
calcFn: var roundTripMiles = distance * 2; var gallonsUsed = roundTripMiles / mpg; var totalFuelCost = gallonsUsed * gasPrice + tolls; var costPerPerson = totalFuelCost / passengers; return {roundTripMiles: fmt(roundTripMiles, 0), gallonsUsed: fmt(gallonsUsed, 1), totalFuelCost: dollar(totalFuelCost), costPerPerson: dollar(costPerPerson)};

faq.q: How do I calculate fuel cost for a trip?
faq.a: Divide total miles by your MPG to get gallons needed, then multiply by the gas price. Add tolls and split between passengers.

faq.q: How can I improve fuel economy on road trips?
faq.a: Drive 60-65 mph instead of 75+, maintain tire pressure, remove roof cargo, and use cruise control on highways.

formulas.name: Fuel Cost
formulas.formula: Cost = (Round Trip Miles / MPG) x Gas Price + Tolls
formulas.explanation: Total distance divided by fuel efficiency gives gallons used, multiplied by price per gallon.

tagline: Optimize character stat allocation for RPG builds
keywords: RPG build optimizer, stat allocation, character build, RPG stats, min-max build
calcFn: var buildBonus = {'DPS (Damage)': {str: 1.3, dex: 1.2, int: 0.8, vit: 0.7}, 'Tank (Defense)': {str: 0.9, dex: 0.7, int: 0.6, vit: 1.8}, 'Healer (Support)': {str: 0.5, dex: 0.7, int: 1.5, vit: 1.3}, 'Balanced': {str: 1, dex: 1, int: 1, vit: 1}, 'Glass Cannon': {str: 1.5, dex: 1.3, int: 1.5, vit: 0.3}}; var bonus = buildBonus[buildType] || buildBonus['Balanced']; var wStr = strength * bonus.str; var wDex = dexterity * bonus.dex; var wInt = intelligence * bonus.int; var wVit = vitality * bonus.vit; var total = wStr + wDex + wInt + wVit; var strPoints = Math.round(totalPoints * (wStr / total)); var dexPoints = Math.round(totalPoints * (wDex / total)); var intPoints = Math.round(totalPoints * (wInt / total)); var vitPoints = totalPoints - strPoints - dexPoints - intPoints; var maxStat = Math.max(strPoints, dexPoints, intPoints, vitPoints); var buildRating = maxStat > totalPoints * 0.5 ? 'Highly Specialized' : maxStat > totalPoints * 0.35 ? 'Focused' : 'Well-Rounded'; return {strPoints: fmt(strPoints, 0), dexPoints: fmt(dexPoints, 0), intPoints: fmt(intPoints, 0), vitPoints: fmt(vitPoints, 0), buildRating: buildRating};

faq.q: What is min-maxing in RPGs?
faq.a: Min-maxing means minimizing less important stats to maximize your primary stats, creating a highly specialized character build.

faq.q: Is a balanced build ever good?
faq.a: Balanced builds are forgiving for beginners and solo play but are usually outperformed by specialized builds in endgame content.

formulas.name: Weighted Allocation
formulas.formula: Points = Total x (Priority x Build Bonus) / Sum of All Weighted Priorities
formulas.explanation: Each stat gets a share of total points proportional to its priority weight multiplied by the build type bonus.

tagline: Calculate how much your gaming setup costs to run
keywords: gaming power cost, PC electricity, gaming electricity, power consumption gaming, gaming energy cost
calcFn: var componentWatts = gpuWatts + cpuWatts + peripherals; var totalDraw = Math.round(componentWatts / (psuEfficiency / 100)); var kwhPerDay = (totalDraw * hoursPerDay) / 1000; var dailyCost = kwhPerDay * electricityRate; var monthlyCost = dailyCost * 30; var annualCost = dailyCost * 365; return {totalDraw: fmt(totalDraw, 0), dailyCost: dollar(dailyCost), monthlyCost: dollar(monthlyCost), annualCost: dollar(annualCost)};

faq.q: How much electricity does a gaming PC use?
faq.a: A mid-range gaming PC draws 300-500 watts while gaming. At average US electricity rates, that costs $15-$30 per month for 4 hours daily.

faq.q: Does PSU efficiency matter for electricity cost?
faq.a: Yes, an 80% efficient PSU wastes 20% of power as heat. Upgrading from 80% to 90% efficiency saves roughly 10% on gaming electricity costs.

formulas.name: Monthly Cost
formulas.formula: Cost = (Total Watts / 1000) x Hours/Day x 30 x Rate
formulas.explanation: Convert watts to kilowatts, multiply by daily hours and monthly days, then by your electricity rate.
